title: ranunculus sardous subsp. intermedius
intro: Ranunculus sardous subsp. intermedius, also known as the lesser clematis, is a flowering plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a purgative and emetic, due to the presence of ranunculin, a toxic compound that induces vomiting. The health benefits of Ranunculus sardous subsp. intermedius include its potential to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by drying the roots and leaves, and the plant is often used in traditional medicine to treat various gastrointestinal issues, although its use should be approached with caution due to its toxic properties.
